brief: This week’s signal environment shows dominant activity in Industry, with concentration in US. Average signal intensity scored 3. Bias skew: neutral.

trend.summary: Signal volume unchanged vs prior week.

articles.title: Kia’s EV sales surged 54% in Q1 with a complete lineup from electric sedans to vans
articles.summary: Kia set a new global sales record in the first quarter, driven by strong demand for electric vehicles. With a full lineup ranging from the entry-level EV2 to the PV5 van, Kia now offers an EV for nearly everyone. more…

articles.title: Electric haul trucks could save Fortescue over $400 million in fuel per year [update]
articles.summary: Fortescue is marching towards zero emissions as it invests in new, zero-emission mining equipment options across its global operations. And that investment? It’s already paying off. One analyst says the company’s saving almost $400 million in fuel costs alone. Each year. UPDATE 26APR2026: $400 million was BEFORE the war in Iran! more…

articles.title: Ford’s 2,200-HP electric Mustang runs 6.87-sec quarter mile, smashes EV record
articles.summary: Ford Racing’s new Mustang Cobra Jet 2200 just became the quickest electric car on the planet, running a 6.87-second quarter mile at 221 mph at the NHRA 4-Wide Nationals in Charlotte this weekend. The run demolished Ford’s own previous EV record — a 7.623-second pass set by the Cobra Jet 1800 in September 2024 — by a massive 0.75 seconds, a staggering improvement in a sport measured in thousandths. more…

articles.summary: A billion-dollar valuation is a headline that’s hard to ignore. And when it’s attached to a new e-bike brand spun out of a company like Rivian, it’s even harder not to get swept up in the excitement. But zoom out for a moment, and a more complicated picture starts to emerge. more…

articles.title: Tesla finally launches ‘Robotaxi’ app on Android, nearly a year after iOS
articles.summary: Tesla has released its “Robotaxi” app on the Google Play Store, making the ride-hailing service available to Android users for the first time. The iOS version launched in September 2025 — putting the Android release nearly a year behind. The app arrived on April 24, just days after Tesla “expanded” its “Robotaxi” service from Austin to Dallas and Houston. more…

articles.title: I went to the Beijing Auto Show and it’s a glimpse at the future of the auto industry
articles.summary: I just spent two days at the 2026 Beijing Auto Show (Auto China 2026), and I need to tell you something: the future of the auto industry is electric and Chinese. I’m not being dramatic. Just realistic. In a single hall at the show, there were more EV models on display than there are available ones in the entire United States. There are 17 halls at this show. Seventeen. And they all have more EVs than the US market. more…

articles.title: XPeng unveils GX flagship SUV with 750 km range, L4-ready hardware for $58,000
articles.summary: XPeng officially unveiled the GX, its new flagship SUV, at the Beijing Auto Show today. The full-size six-seater starts at 399,800 yuan (~$58,000) and packs up to 750 km (466 miles) of AWD range in its pure electric version. The GX represents XPeng’s most ambitious push into the premium market yet, bringing together the company’s most advanced autonomous driving hardware, a Bosch steer-by-wire system, and aviation-grade safety redundancy — all in a package that directly undercuts European luxury SUVs. more…

articles.title: CATL says sodium batteries are mainstream-ready, signs massive 60 GWh deal
articles.summary: CATL, the world’s largest battery maker, signed a 60 GWh sodium-ion battery deal with energy storage integrator HyperStrong — the largest sodium-ion battery order ever placed. The three-year agreement is equivalent to half of all energy storage batteries CATL delivered in 2025. The deal marks what CATL calls proof that it has “overcome the challenges of the entire sodium-ion battery mass production chain.” Industry observers are already calling it a potential “DeepSeek moment” for the global energy storage industry. more…

articles.title: EIA: 80 GW of new solar, wind + storage capacity coming in 2026
articles.summary: Utility-scale solar, wind, and battery storage will add more than 80 gigawatts (GW) of new generating capacity in the US by February 28, 2027, while total fossil fuel and nuclear power capacity will fall by almost 5 GW, according to data just released by the US Energy Information Administration (EIA), which was reviewed by the SUN DAY Campaign. more…

articles.title: First drive: next-gen Xiaomi SU7 shows how fast Chinese EVs are improving
articles.summary: I got behind the wheel of the next-generation Xiaomi SU7, and the upgrades are significant. The refreshed electric sedan delivers up to 902 km (560 miles) of CLTC range, 800V charging architecture across all trims, and standard LiDAR — all while still undercutting the Tesla Model 3 in China. Xiaomi started deliveries of its updated best-seller in April after securing over 100,000 pre-orders, building on the 381,000 first-gen SU7s sold since the original launch in March 2024. more…

articles.title: Tesla files to deliver Elon Musk’s $56 billion pay package – ending the saga
articles.summary: Tesla has filed an S-8 registration statement with the SEC to register 303,960,630 shares of common stock for CEO Elon Musk under his 2018 pay package. At today’s share price of ~$376, those shares are worth over $114 billion. The filing confirms what many expected after the Delaware Supreme Court restored the award in December: the years-long legal fight over the largest executive compensation deal in corporate history is officially over. more…

articles.title: BYD upgrades one of its top selling EVs with 5-min flash charging and 390 miles range
articles.summary: BYD launched its new Flash Charging system, enabling EV charging in as little as 5 minutes, in luxury vehicles like the Denza Z9 GT. Now, it’s set to roll out to some of its best sellers. more…

articles.title: Wheel-E Podcast: Cargo e-bikes, pedal-by-wire, Yozma IN10, laws, more
articles.summary: This week on Electrek’s Wheel-E podcast, we discuss the most popular news stories from the world of electric bikes and other nontraditional electric vehicles. This time, that includes Segway’s new cargo bikes, pedal-by-wire drive systems, semi-solid-state batteries for electric bicycles, more laws on the way, Yozma IN10 review, and more.  more…

articles.title: Hyundai unveils sleek new IONIQ V: a production car that looks like a concept
articles.summary: Hyundai unveiled the IONIQ V at Auto China 2026 in Beijing today, a sleek electric liftback with over 600 km of CLTC range built on an 800V platform with CATL batteries. It’s the production version of the Venus concept the automaker revealed earlier this month. The IONIQ V is the first dedicated IONIQ production model for the Chinese market, and it marks the beginning of an aggressive 20-model offensive as Hyundai tries to claw back relevance in the world’s largest EV market. more…

articles.title: Porsche Cayenne Coupe Electric is an off-road capable, 2.4s 0-60mph beauty and beast
articles.summary: Porsche announced today that the Cayenne Coupe Electric would join the ICE and hybrid Coupe varieties.  With it some truly amazing specs for an SUV in Coupe clothing throws out up to 850 kW (1139 hp) 1106 ft.-lbs. torque with a 2.4 second 0-60mph and top speed of 162 mph. But it is also quite the stunner as well… more…

articles.title: Interview: we talk home batteries with FranklinWH CEO Gary Lam
articles.summary: On today’s charged up episode of Quick Charge, we’ve got FranklinWH co-founder and CEO Gary Lam here to talk us through some of the benefits of a home battery system, and what it takes to make the most of one – with, or even without a home solar panel system. more…
